questions.explanation: The expression 2⁵ means 2 multiplied by itself 5 times: 2 × 2 × 2 × 2 × 2 = 32. It is not 2 × 5.
The square root of 81 is the number that, when multiplied by itself, equals 81. Since 9 × 9 = 81, the correct answer is 9.
According to the index laws, when you multiply powers with the same base, you add the exponents. So, 3² × 3⁴ = 3²⁺⁴ = 3⁶.
According to the index laws, when you divide powers with the same base, you subtract the exponents. So, 10³ ÷ 10⁵ = 10³⁻⁵ = 10⁻².

options: It is not possible to calculate.
questions.explanation: Any non-zero number raised to the power of 0 is equal to 1.
The number 4 can be written as 4¹. Using the division index law, 4³ ÷ 4¹ = 4³⁻¹ = 4².
According to the index laws, when you raise a power to another power, you multiply the exponents. So, (2³)² = 2³ˣ² = 2⁶.
A number raised to the power of 1/2 is equivalent to finding its square root. The square root of 9 is 3.
A number raised to the power of 1/3 is equivalent to finding its cube root. The cube root of 27 is 3, because 3 × 3 × 3 = 27.
A negative index indicates the reciprocal of the number. So, 2⁻³ = 1/2³ = 1/8.

questions.questionText: The area of a square is 64 cm². What is the length of one side?
questions.explanation: The area of a square is side length squared (s²). To find the side length, you need to find the square root of the area. √64 = 8 cm.
The negative index means a reciprocal. 10⁻⁴ = 1/10⁴ = 1/10000 = 0.0001.
